question: What is the full list of hook events a charm can observe? List them all.

claude_output.result:
Here is the full list of hook events a charm can observe, organized by category:

---

## ⚙️ Lifecycle Hooks

| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `install` | Runs once, before any other hook, when the unit is first deployed |
| `config-changed` | Fired after `install`, after `upgrade-charm`, and whenever config values change |
| `start` | Fired after `install` + `config-changed`; signals the unit is ready |
| `upgrade-charm` | Fired after the charm code has been upgraded |
| `update-status` | Fired automatically by Juju at regular intervals (heartbeat) |
| `stop` | Fired before unit removal begins |
| `remove` | The last event a unit ever sees before being torn down |

---

## 👑 Leadership Hooks

| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `leader-elected` | Fired on the unit that has just become leader |
| `leader-settings-changed` | Fired on all non-leader units when the leader writes settings |

---

## 🔗 Relation Hooks
*(prefix `<endpoint>` is the name of the relation endpoint)*

| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `<endpoint>-relation-created` | Fired when a relation is first established between two applications |
| `<endpoint>-relation-joined` | Fired when a remote unit joins the relation |
| `<endpoint>-relation-changed` | Fired when a remote unit's relation data changes |
| `<endpoint>-relation-departed` | Fired when a remote unit leaves the relation |
| `<endpoint>-relation-broken` | Fired when the entire relation is being removed |

---

## 💾 Storage Hooks
*(prefix `<storage>` is the name of the storage definition)*

| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `<storage>-storage-attached` | Fired when a storage instance becomes available to the unit |
| `<storage>-storage-detaching` | Fired before a storage instance is removed |

---

## 🔐 Secret Hooks

| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `secret-changed` | Fired on a secret **consumer** when a new revision is available |
| `secret-rotate` | Fired on the secret **owner** when it's time to rotate the secret |
| `secret-expired` | Fired on the secret **owner** when a revision reaches its expiry time |
| `secret-remove` | Fired on the secret **owner** when an old revision can be safely deleted |

---

## 🐳 Workload / Pebble Hooks (Kubernetes only)
*(prefix `<container>` is the name of the container)*

| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `<container>-pebble-ready` | Fired when the Pebble API in the container becomes available |
| `<container>-pebble-custom-notice` | Fired when a custom Pebble notice is emitted |
| `<container>-pebble-check-failed` | Fired when a Pebble health check transitions to failing |
| `<container>-pebble-check-recovered` | Fired when a previously failing Pebble health check recovers |

---

## 🔄 Series Upgrade Hooks (machine charms)

| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `pre-series-upgrade` | Fired before an OS series upgrade begins |
| `post-series-upgrade` | Fired after the OS series upgrade completes |

---

That covers **all** hook events. The most commonly used in day-to-day charm development are the lifecycle hooks, relation hooks, and (for Kubernetes charms) the Pebble hooks. Secret hooks are newer and increasingly important for charms that manage credentials.
